## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (complete hub design) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG (hub/workspace) - **Scope name:** Anthropic Internal Knowledge & Safety Hub - **Scope description:**

A single place for mission-first updates, safety policy acknowledgements, and working guidance across Anthropic.

- **Welcome message:**

Welcome to the Anthropic Internal Hub. Use this space to stay aligned on Claude launches, responsible scaling, governance requirements, and the day-to-day documentation that keeps teams moving safely.

**Scope hero image (brand logo URL from your profile; uploaded later in Phase 1.5):** - Logo.dev URL: https://img.logo.dev/anthropic.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true **Brand colors (from profile mapping):** - **primary_color:** #D4A27F - **primary_color_contrast:** #191919 - **secondary_color:** #191919 - **warn_color:** #C0392B - **warn_color_contrast:** #FFFFFF **Highlights (hub homepage):** - number_of_highlights: **4** - highlights_carousel: **true** - highlights_style: **BannerCarousel** **Footer (3 columns with real links from profile):** - Column 1: - About / Company: https://www.anthropic.com/news - Careers: https://www.anthropic.com/careers - Column 2: - Research: https://www.anthropic.com/research - Column 3: - Responsible Scaling Policy: https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy - Safety: https://www.anthropic.com/safety **Social links (from profile):** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/anthropicresearch - Twitter / X: https://twitter.com/AnthropicAI - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@anthropic-ai **navigation_links** - **topBarLinks (3):** 1) Newsroom: https://www.anthropic.com/news 2) Responsible Scaling Policy: https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy 3) Developer Docs: https://docs.anthropic.com - **footerLinks (5):** - Careers: https://www.anthropic.com/careers - Research: https://www.anthropic.com/research - Safety: https://www.anthropic.com/safety - Responsible Scaling Policy: https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-scaling-policy - Long-Term Benefit Trust: https://www.anthropic.com/news/the-long-term-benefit-trust - **socialLinks:** LinkedIn, Twitter/X, YouTube (as above) **components (pick 1–3 widgets):** - recentDocuments (knowledge/document-heavy org behavior around policies, papers, and system cards) - upcomingEvents (town halls / launches / training sessions) - workAnniversaries (culture continuity as teams scale) --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels total) I’m using **3 PRIMARY** channels (sticky, most content) and **2 SECONDARY** channels (not sticky, hide_highlights=true). #### Ch1 (PRIMARY): “Claude Launch Notes” - **Purpose:** Launch recaps and operational notes for Claude, Claude Code, and related enterprise releases. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #D4A27F - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url (stock match):** stock/Innovation.png - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** Where do I find the latest “what changed” summary for a model release? **A:** Use the “Claude Launch Notes” pinned posts and release recaps; each includes a short changelog, safety implications, and rollout guidance. 2) **Q:** Are there different guidance notes for Claude API vs Claude.ai? **A:** Yes—deployment and evaluation notes differ by surface. The channel highlights include the relevant scope sections for each product path. 3) **Q:** When do ASL-3 / Responsible Scaling gates apply? **A:** For production model launches, the Responsible Scaling Policy gates determine readiness. See the pinned policy acknowledgement in “Responsible Scaling & Governance”. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML, 2–3 sentences):**

Launch recaps written in Anthropic’s internal voice: specific, evidence-conscious, and honest about trade-offs.

Find what shipped, what was tested, and what teams should do next—whether you’re integrating via the Claude API, updating internal tooling, or planning a rollout.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch2 (PRIMARY): “Responsible Scaling & Governance” - **Purpose:** Policy acknowledgements, governance updates, and read-confirmation-required requirements. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #C0392B - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** stock/audit.png - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** Do I need to re-acknowledge policy changes after an update? **A:** When a policy changes materially, acknowledgements are reissued. Use the pinned “Policy Acknowledgements” item to confirm current requirements. 2) **Q:** What is the Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) and why does it affect releases? **A:** RSP versioned safeguards create formal deployment gates; Anthropic only trains or deploys unless adequate safeguards are in place. 3) **Q:** How does the Long-Term Benefit Trust (LTBT) influence governance? **A:** The LTBT is legally designed to prevent mission drift under commercial pressure, including electing board power over time. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML):**

Safety and governance aren’t side quests here—they’re part of how we decide what ships, when, and under what constraints.

This channel hosts policy acknowledgements and governance updates. Many posts require read confirmation.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' #### Ch3 (PRIMARY): “People, Culture & Work Rhythm” - **Purpose:** Hiring growth context, working norms, and culture signals as teams scale rapidly. - **Type:** public - **Color:** #E8C9A8 - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** stock/community.png - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** What’s expected for collaboration in small autonomous teams as headcount triples? **A:** Teams prioritize high-trust, low-ego collaboration—decisions are made by the people closest to the work, with explicit evidence-sharing. 2) **Q:** What’s the work model expectation across locations? **A:** Hybrid expectations are role-dependent, with SF as the centre of gravity for key leadership decisions. Use the pinned “Work Model” guide. 3) **Q:** Are career ladders/promotion criteria changing with growth pains? **A:** Yes—growth has exposed scaling gaps. Posts in this channel explain what’s changing and how to align your expectations. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML):**

As Anthropic scales fast, we still try to keep the operating principle intact: mission primacy, high-trust collaboration, and evidence-based reasoning.

Use this channel for practical culture guidance—so new teams can ramp quickly without losing the actual norms that make the work effective.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch4 (SECONDARY): “Security & Systems Hygiene” - **Purpose:** Security guidance, password practice, and required read confirmations for IT/security changes. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #191919 - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** stock/security.png - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** What should I do if I suspect a phishing or account compromise? **A:** Follow the incident response steps in the pinned security notice and complete required read confirmation updates. 2) **Q:** Why are password and access practices emphasized for AI systems and integrations? **A:** Access hygiene reduces the risk of both account-level compromise and downstream toolchain exposure. 3) **Q:** Are there mandatory controls tied to production deployments? **A:** Yes. Security requirements often map to deployment readiness and Responsible Scaling gates. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description (HTML):**

Security doesn’t belong to a separate universe. It’s part of our operational safety—especially when systems connect to external tools, codebases, and enterprise workflows.

Many items in this channel require read confirmation. Treat them like operational gates.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' #### Ch5 (SECONDARY): “Policy & Compliance Acknowledgements” - **Purpose:** Legal/compliance summaries and “read before proceeding” requirements. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** #C0392B - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** stock/law.png - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** What kinds of posts require me to acknowledge before work? **A:** Policy, legal, compliance, and security/security-adjacent changes—especially those affecting training, content use, or deployment conditions. 2) **Q:** How do we handle training-related obligations and disputes? **A:** Use the pinned acknowledgements; they reference what we must follow operationally based on current policy posture. 3) **Q:** Where do I find the Responsible Scaling linkage to compliance posture? **A:** The linkage is documented in “Responsible Scaling & Governance” and mirrored in the compliance acknowledgement notes. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description (HTML):**

This channel captures legal and compliance acknowledgements in plain language, written for people who will actually use them.

When a post requires confirmation, it’s not optional—please acknowledge promptly so teams can keep working without ambiguity.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (policies with require_read_confirmation + 1–2 Events) **Events:** 2 total across the hub. **Main highlights:** exactly 4 items with is_main_highlight=True. > Note: because you instructed “derive from the profile, never invent,” I’m careful to only use facts/dates/numbers explicitly present in the profile. For “body length,” I’ll write substantive policy/ops guidance that references the stated milestones without inventing new org-specific operational procedures. #### Ch1 (Claude Launch Notes) — 3 items (PRIMARY) 1) **Title:** Claude Code crossed $2.5B annualised revenue — what we’re changing in enterprise readiness - Type: **spotlight** - tags: ['claude-code', 'enterprise', 'growth', 'deployments'] - is_main_highlight: **True** (1/4) - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/financial_report.png - Body outline: recap the $2.5B figure, connect to agentic adoption, and outline rollout/readiness focuses tied to enterprise growth. 2) **Title:** Hybrid reasoning in Claude 4 — what Opus 4 changed for long-horizon tasks - Type: **guide** - tags: ['claude-4', 'reasoning', 'opus-4', 'product'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/Decision.png - Body outline: explain hybrid reasoning mode conceptually, relate Opus 4 SWE-bench Verified 72.5%, and provide internal “how to evaluate” guidance without inventing metrics beyond what’s given. 3) **Title:** Cowork rollout update — lessons from PwC’s training and underwriting cycle improvements - Type: **recap** - tags: ['cowork', 'pwc', 'agents', 'workflows'] - is_main_highlight: **True** (2/4) - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/warehouse.png - Body outline: summarize Cowork’s purpose, mention the PwC partnership and the documented underwriting improvement (10 weeks → 10 days), plus “what to measure” suggestions. --- #### Ch2 (Responsible Scaling & Governance) — 3 items (PRIMARY) 1) **Title:** Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) 3.1 — how ASL-3 gates work in practice for launches - Type: **policy** - tags: ['rsp', 'asl-3', 'safety', 'deployment-gates'] - is_main_highlight: **True** (3/4) - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/security.png - Body outline: connect RSP 3.1 to operational gating, explicitly reference that ASL-3 activated first time May 2025 and that ASL-3 created production deployment consequences. 2) **Title:** Long-Term Benefit Trust (LTBT) update — trustee Vas Narasimhan (April 2026) and governance implications - Type: **milestone** - tags: ['ltbt', 'governance', 'board', 'mission'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/leadership_speech.png - Body outline: summarize LTBT purpose, mention trustees and board majority trajectory, and frame “why governance decisions matter as much as models we ship.” 3) **Title:** Copyright litigation settlement posture — what we’re required to acknowledge and document internally - Type: **policy** - tags: ['copyright', 'compliance', 'risk', 'acknowledgement'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/law.png - Body outline: reference the $1.5B settlement figure and the ongoing music publishers case described; focus on acknowledgement and documentation expectations. --- #### Ch3 (People, Culture & Work Rhythm) — 3 items (PRIMARY) 1) **Title:** Tripling the workforce in 2026 — keeping mission primacy as teams scale - Type: **milestone** - tags: ['culture', 'growth', 'collaboration', 'mission'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/team_diversity.png - Body outline: use profile’s “mission primacy,” “high-trust low-ego,” “growth pains,” and “no layoffs” facts to set expectations. 2) **Title:** Work model at scale — hybrid expectations with SF as the centre of gravity - Type: **guide** - tags: ['hybrid', 'sf', 'locations', 'work-model'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/office.png - Body outline: reference SF HQ, London, Dublin, NYC, and Bengaluru planned for 2026; provide practical guidance without inventing team-by-team rules. 3) **Title:** Compensation and equity donation matching — why internal equity stays predictable while scaling - Type: **spotlight** - tags: ['compensation', 'equity', 'benefits', 'equity-donation'] - is_main_highlight: **True** (4/4) - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/savings.png - Body outline: summarize level-based pay with no negotiation variance, and the 1:1 equity donation matching up to 25%. --- #### Ch4 (Security & Systems Hygiene) — 2 items (SECONDARY) 1) **Title:** Password & account hygiene refresh — practical controls for a model+toolchain world - Type: **guide** - tags: ['security', 'passwords', 'access', 'hygiene'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/Password.png - Body outline: security rationale for toolchain exposure; includes acknowledgement language (no invented procedures—keep it conceptual and policy-aligned). 2) **Title:** Incident-response readiness — what “suspicion” should trigger in your workflow - Type: **policy** - tags: ['incident-response', 'risk', 'security', 'process'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/risk.png - Body outline: explain how readiness reduces ambiguity; reference that Responsible Scaling creates operational gates (link back to governance channel). --- #### Ch5 (Policy & Compliance Acknowledgements) — 2 items (SECONDARY) 1) **Title:** Responsible Scaling & compliance linkage — acknowledging how gates affect deployment scope - Type: **policy** - tags: ['compliance', 'rsp', 'safety', 'deployment'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/agreement.png - Body outline: tie compliance acknowledgement to RSP and mission integrity. 2) **Title (EVENT): Town Hall on Governance + Product Readiness — ask about LTBT, ASL-3, and enterprise deployment - Type: **event** - tags: ['town-hall', 'governance', 'safety', 'product'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/video_call.png - event_start/event_end/location: **Need you to confirm exact datetime + location** (profile contains no event schedule details) --- ### Event timing blocker (must clarify before build) You instructed to include 1–2 Events with **ISO datetimes**. The profile does **not** contain any specific internal event date/time/location. **Please provide either:** 1) Exact datetime + timezone for **this Town Hall** (Ch5), and optionally location text, **or** 2) Tell me to create Events with a specific placeholder date you choose (e.g., “2026-06-15T17:00:00Z”)—but I understand your “never invent,” so you likely need to supply the dates. Right now I can’t safely set event_start/event_end. --- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH - **company_name:** Anthropic - **website_name:** anthopic? (from profile: https://www.anthropic.com) - I will set website_name exactly as **Anthropic** (or **anthropic.com**) only if you confirm which you want—your build instructions say to use “from profile,” but doesn’t explicitly state a “website_name” field value besides the domain. - **default_language:** **en-US** (HQ in San Francisco, USA) - **supported_languages:** [“en-US”] by default (profile lists London/Dublin/India but does not explicitly confirm language tags; if you want, we can add “en-GB” and “en-IE” based on locations, but that would be an inference—tell me if that’s acceptable) - **login_quick_links (3–5 real URLs from profile):** 1) Careers: https://www.anthropic.com/careers 2) Newsroom: https://www.anthropic.com/news 3) Research: https://www.anthropic.com/research 4) Developer Docs: https://docs.anthropic.com 5) Claude: https://claude.ai - **sidebar_navigation (ordered by priorities):** 1) collaborators (mission-first collaboration + small teams) 2) documents (policies, RSP, governance, compliance) 3) approvalRequests (acknowledgements requiring read confirmation) 4) workgroups (ERGs/affinity networks implied; growth needs structure) 5) learning (upskilling, launch-readiness guidance) --- ## Confirmation needed before I can build This is a complete design. **Before we proceed**, two choices are missing: 1) **Events:** Please provide **event_start + event_end (ISO 8601) and location text** for the Town Hall in Ch5 (and any second event if you want 2 events total). 2) **website_name:** should it be **“Anthropic”** or **“anthropic.com”**? **This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, content plan, colors, hub settings, and the event dates?**

arguments.name: Anthropic Internal Knowledge & Safety Hub
arguments.description: Anthropic is an AI safety company that builds and deploys large language models under the Claude brand. This internal hub centralizes mission-first updates, policy acknowledgements, and the working guidance teams need to ship safely at scale.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to the Anthropic Internal Hub. Use this space to stay aligned on Claude launches, responsible scaling, governance requirements, and the day-to-day documentation that keeps teams moving safely.

channels.name: Claude Launch Notes
channels.description:
Launch recaps written in Anthropic’s internal voice: specific, evidence-conscious, and honest about trade-offs.

Find what shipped, what was tested, and what teams should do next—whether you’re integrating via the Claude API, updating internal tooling, or planning a rollout.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the latest “what changed” summary for a model release?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the “Claude Launch Notes” pinned posts and release recaps; each includes a short changelog, safety implications, and rollout guidance.

frequent_questions.question: Are there different guidance notes for Claude API vs Claude.ai?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes—deployment and evaluation notes differ by surface. The channel highlights include the relevant scope sections for each product path.

frequent_questions.question: When do ASL-3 / Responsible Scaling gates apply?
frequent_questions.answer: For production model launches, the Responsible Scaling Policy gates determine readiness. See the pinned policy acknowledgement in “Responsible Scaling & Governance”.

channels.name: Responsible Scaling & Governance
channels.description:
Safety and governance aren’t side quests here—they’re part of how we decide what ships, when, and under what constraints.

This channel hosts policy acknowledgements and governance updates. Many posts require read confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: Do I need to re-acknowledge policy changes after an update?
frequent_questions.answer: When a policy changes materially, acknowledgements are reissued. Use the pinned “Policy Acknowledgements” item to confirm current requirements.

frequent_questions.question: What is the Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) and why does it affect releases?
frequent_questions.answer: RSP versioned safeguards create formal deployment gates; Anthropic only trains or deploys unless adequate safeguards are in place.

frequent_questions.question: How does the Long-Term Benefit Trust (LTBT) influence governance?
frequent_questions.answer: The LTBT is legally designed to prevent mission drift under commercial pressure, including electing board power over time.

channels.name: People, Culture & Work Rhythm
channels.description:
As Anthropic scales fast, we still try to keep the operating principle intact: mission primacy, high-trust collaboration, and evidence-based reasoning.

Use this channel for practical culture guidance—so new teams can ramp quickly without losing the actual norms that make the work effective.

frequent_questions.question: What’s expected for collaboration in small autonomous teams as headcount triples?
frequent_questions.answer: Teams prioritize high-trust, low-ego collaboration—decisions are made by the people closest to the work, with explicit evidence-sharing.

frequent_questions.question: What’s the work model expectation across locations?
frequent_questions.answer: Hybrid expectations are role-dependent, with SF as the centre of gravity for key leadership decisions. Use the pinned “Work Model” guide.

frequent_questions.question: Are career ladders/promotion criteria changing with growth pains?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes—growth has exposed scaling gaps. Posts in this channel explain what’s changing and how to align your expectations.

channels.name: Security & Systems Hygiene
channels.description:
Security doesn’t belong to a separate universe. It’s part of our operational safety—especially when systems connect to external tools, codebases, and enterprise workflows.

Many items in this channel require read confirmation. Treat them like operational gates.

frequent_questions.question: What should I do if I suspect a phishing or account compromise?
frequent_questions.answer: Follow the incident response steps in the pinned security notice and complete required read confirmation updates.

frequent_questions.question: Why are password and access practices emphasized for AI systems and integrations?
frequent_questions.answer: Access hygiene reduces the risk of both account-level compromise and downstream toolchain exposure.

frequent_questions.question: Are there mandatory controls tied to production deployments?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes. Security requirements often map to deployment readiness and Responsible Scaling gates.

channels.name: Policy & Compliance Acknowledgements
channels.description:
This channel captures legal and compliance acknowledgements in plain language, written for people who will actually use them.

When a post requires confirmation, it’s not optional—please acknowledge promptly so teams can keep working without ambiguity.

frequent_questions.question: What kinds of posts require me to acknowledge before work?
frequent_questions.answer: Policy, legal, compliance, and security/security-adjacent changes—especially those affecting training, content use, or deployment conditions.

frequent_questions.question: How do we handle training-related obligations and disputes?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the pinned acknowledgements; they reference what we must follow operationally based on current policy posture.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the Responsible Scaling linkage to compliance posture?
frequent_questions.answer: The linkage is documented in “Responsible Scaling & Governance” and mirrored in the compliance acknowledgement notes.
